title: Router Table Dust Collection Box
summary: Upgrade your Kreg router lift with a dust collection system that captures virtually all of the dust created on the router table!
description: Upgrade your Kreg router lift with a dust collection system that captures virtually all of the dust created on the router table!

materials.name: Box of 1 ¼” pocket hole screws (See tools list)

steps.title: Watch the video
steps.body: Before attempting this project, watch the full youtube video to get a better understanding of how to complete the project. Link in Extras below.

steps.title: Cut plywood
steps.body: Cut all pieces according to the cutlist. Note that all of the pieces on this project are made out of ⅝” plywood but you can substitute different thickness if you adjust dimensions properly.

steps.title: Attach side, bottom, and back pieces together
steps.body: Using pocket holes, attach all of the side pieces together securely

steps.title: Cut out a hole for the dust port
steps.body: Use a drill to drill some pilot holes in the back of the dust box, then cut out the rest of the hole using a jigsaw

steps.title: Attach dust collection port
steps.body: After cutting out the hole in step 3, attach the dust collection port with screws or bolts to secure it to the back of the box

steps.title: Cut door panel
steps.body: Grabbing the extra front panel that you cut earlier, cut the panel roughly in half to create a door for the dust collection box

steps.title: Drill hinge cups
steps.body: Using the Kreg Concealed Hinge Jig, drill out cups for the concealed hinges to sit in

steps.title: Attach door to dust box
steps.body: Once the concealed hinges are attach to the door, attach the opposite side of the hinges to the dust box to create a functional door

steps.title: Attach dust box to router table
steps.body: With the door attached, using pocket hole screws to attach the dust collection box underneath the router table and you are done!
